conditions: EPDM and TPO are different polymer families (thermoset and thermoplastic respectively). Direct heat welding is not possible between the two materials. Contact is physically compatible — no chemical degradation occurs. However, adhesive bonding at transitions requires manufacturer-approved transition strips or adhesives designed for dissimilar membrane splicing. Mechanical attachment with compression bars is acceptable at transitions.
notes: Common scenario in re-roofing where new TPO membrane is installed adjacent to existing EPDM membrane. No chemical incompatibility in contact, but bonding between dissimilar polymers requires mechanical or adhesive transition methods.
